stories.headline: Iran Rushes to Woo Asia’s Largest Oil Importers After US Waiver
stories.summary: Iran is racing to court some of Asia’s largest oil buyers as a 60-day US sanctions waiver takes effect, a temporary lifeline that should allow Tehran to resume exports and begin clearing a backlog of cargoes on the water, even as peace talks continue.

stories.headline: Football offers escape for Gaza, but World Cup spirit dimmed
stories.summary: Palestinians in Gaza struggle to find World Cup joy amid displacement, power outages, and memories of previous lives.

stories.headline: Extreme heat: is the UK becoming a 40C country? – podcast
stories.summary: Met Office forecasters have issued a rare red weather warning for England, with temperatures potentially reaching 40C (104F) in some places. Europe is also dealing with a debilitating heatwave, with schools closed, trains cancelled and France even restricting the consumption of alcohol outdoors to take pressure off the emergency services. The high temperatures coincide with the coming El Niño, which some scientists have nicknamed Godzilla for its

stories.headline: Conservative radio host defends Trump, says he’s better than Vance
stories.summary: Prominent conservative radio host, spoke at the International Policy Summit to defend Trump’s handling of the Iran war.

stories.headline: Vance says direct talks with Iran set 'good foundation' for deal to end the war
stories.summary: Lower-level officials are continuing to engage in what officials described as “technical talks” in Switzerland following Vance, Ghalibaf and Araghchi’s departure, as the US and Iran look to inch closer to a concrete deal.

stories.headline: China Stocks in Hong Kong Eye Bear Market on Spending Woes
stories.summary: Chinese equities in Hong Kong edged toward a bear market as sentiment soured on tepid consumer spending and fading confidence in e-commerce firms.

stories.headline: Deutsche Bank Cuts Gold Forecasts up to 22% as Bulls Temper View
stories.summary: Deutsche Bank AG reduced its gold-price forecasts by as much as 22%, as investors become more wary about the outlook for US monetary policy and investment demand for the precious metal dries up.

stories.headline: Greenspan’s Legacy Is as Convoluted as His Words
stories.summary: His reputation no longer lies in post-crisis tatters, but should it inspire Trump 2.0?

stories.headline: Collapsed Surfside Condo Had Been Failing for Weeks, Investigators Find
stories.summary: Two columns that held up the building’s pool deck began to fail several weeks before the 2021 collapse, according to a new federal report.

stories.summary: Kazakhstan's president has arrived in Brussels this week to meet EU leaders and assess the delivery of promises made at last year's EU-Central Asia summit in Samarkand, with the Middle Corridor trade route connecting China to Europe through Central Asia at the top of the agenda.

stories.headline: Federal judge blocks Trump's push to use database to check citizenship status prior to voting
stories.summary: US federal court says the Trump-led initiative carries the risk of preventing valid voters from their constitutional right to democracy and tramples on Americans’ privacy.

stories.headline: Australia's coal and gas exports violate our human rights, group says in new UN case
stories.summary: The group says that it is unlawful for Australia to continue approving fossil fuel exports without protecting its citizens.

Korean Stocks Tumble 8% From Record, Triggering Trading Halt
stories.summary: South Korean stocks tumbled from a record high as investors sold off chip heavyweights on renewed concern that the rally has become overstretched.

stories.headline: Zerodha's Kamath Backs Indian Stocks, Boosts Allocation
stories.summary: Zerodha co-founder, Nikhil Kamath, says he's increasing exposure to Indian equities despite recent foreign outflows and muted market performance. He joined Bloomberg's Minmin Low on the sidelines of the World Economic Forum in Dalian and appeared on Insight with Haslinda Amin. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Pure Lithium's CEO Bodoin on Battery Technology
stories.summary: Emilie Bodoin, Founder, Chairman & CEO of Pure Lithium, discusses advances in battery technology and supply chains. The company has a pilot line launching in Chicago and is seeking partners for commercialization. She speaks on the sidelines of the World Economic Forum in Dalian with Bloomberg’s Minmin Low. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Novonesis on Business Strategy
stories.summary: Ester Baiget, CEO of Danish biosolutions company Novonesis, discusses the firm's China business and growth strategy. She speaks with Minmin Low from the sidelines of the World Economic Forum's Annual Meeting of the New Champions in Dalian. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Nomura Joins Sony in Powering Big Week for Japanese Bond Sales
stories.summary: Nomura Holdings Inc and Sony Group Corp. are leading a slew of deals from Asia in global bonds markets on Tuesday, in what is shaping up to be one of the biggest weeks this year overseas for Japanese issuers.

stories.headline: Baht Drops to One-Year Low on Expectations of Rate Gap With US
stories.summary: The baht falls to a one-year low on expectations of a wider interest rate gap between the US and Thailand as domestic demand comes under pressure from multiple headwinds.

stories.headline: SK Hynix ETF’s Assets Soar to $17 Billion, Biggest in Hong Kong
stories.summary: A leveraged exchange traded product tied to SK Hynix Inc. has amassed more than $16.8 billion in assets under management, making it Hong Kong’s largest ETF and underscoring investors’ voracious appetite for risky semiconductor bets.

stories.headline: Tech Tumble Overshadows Markets' Iran Hopes | The Asia Trade 6/23/2026
stories.summary: "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Paul Allen, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Global Funds Snap Indian Stock Selling Streak as Oil Shock Ebbs
stories.summary: Foreign investors turned buyers of Indian equities for the first time in two months as easing Middle East tensions and lower oil prices revive appetite for the market.

stories.headline: Japan’s Five-Year Bond Sale Draws Soft Demand on Rate Hike Fears
stories.summary: Japan’s five-year government bond auction Tuesday saw lower demand than the 12-month average, as yen weakness added to expectations that the Bank of Japan may need to raise rates faster.

stories.headline: At least 13 killed and dozens injured after Qatar gas explosion
stories.summary: The city's main liquified natural gas (LNG) processing site suffered a "technical accident" in the Ras Laffan industrial zone.

stories.headline: EU 're-assessing' summit with UK after Keir Starmer lays out exit plan
stories.summary: A European Commission spokesperson said on Monday that the EU is "reassessing" plans to hold a summit with the UK in late July, as it becomes increasingly likely that Prime Minister Keir Starmer's successor will be in post in just three and a half weeks.

stories.headline: Alan Greenspan, architect of the modern American economy, dies aged 100
stories.summary: As chairman of the Federal Reserve, Alan Greenspan became the world's most high-profile banker.

stories.headline: Oil Extends Decline as Iran Waiver Signals Peace Talks Progress
stories.summary: Oil extended declines on signs of progress toward an agreement to end the Iran war, which included a US waiver allowing some sales of crude and fuels from the Islamic Republic.

stories.headline: Federal Citizenship Data Tool Cannot Be Used to Screen Voters, Judge Rules
stories.summary: A federal judge ruled that the Trump administration’s providing federal data to states to check and purge their voter rolls violated several laws prohibiting the disclosure.

stories.headline: Congressional Democrats Warn Pulte Against Mass Firings
stories.summary: Current and former officials say the acting director of national intelligence is planning to announce major cuts to his office as early as Monday.

stories.headline: Embattled Superintendent of Los Angeles School District Resigns
stories.summary: Alberto Carvalho resigned Sunday, months after the F.B.I. raided his home and office. He came to Los Angeles from Miami with a reputation for raising student test scores but also made missteps.

stories.headline: 'Another day, another leader': Londoners react to Starmer's resignation
stories.summary: People in central London expressed frustration, uncertainty and cautious hope after Keir Starmer announced his resignation as UK prime minister.

stories.headline: Kenya minister found in contempt of court over US-backed Ebola centre

stories.headline: Clive Davis, music mogul behind Whitney Houston and Bruce Springsteen, dies aged 94
stories.summary: The record company executive also worked with Billy Joel, Pink Floyd, Alicia Keys and Aerosmith.

stories.headline: Trump-backed political outsider wins Colombia election, initial count shows
stories.summary: Abelardo de la Espriella appears to have narrowly defeated his rival Iván Cepeda, who says the preliminary count is "not yet official or binding".

stories.headline: Guinea bans exports of raw gold to boost local refining
stories.summary: The prohibition is intended to boost the country's economy by refining gold domestically.

stories.headline: China's import of custard apples is sparking fears in Taiwan
stories.summary: Taiwan's agriculture ministry is worried that Beijing wants to weaponise a local specialty: the atemoya.

stories.headline: Protest led by India's 'cockroach party' enters its third day

stories.headline: Sony Plans First Dollar-Bond Sale in Nearly Three Decades
stories.summary: Sony Group Corp. was still marketing the original PlayStation when it last tapped the US investment-grade bond market. Almost three decades later, it’s readying a comeback.

stories.summary: A weaker rupee and tougher immigration rules are pushing Indian students away from popular destinations like the US and UK.

stories.headline: Bolivian president declares state of emergency

stories.summary: The leaders have asked for apologies from the countries that benefited from the slave trade, as well as debt relief and financial compensation.
